brand_info.name: LUZU TV
brand_info.tagline: Estamos dónde hay que estar. Somos lo que queremos ser.
brand_info.positioning: A leading independent streaming service that connects emotionally with a young audience, fostering an active and committed community through authentic, dynamic, and participatory content, breaking with traditional media standards.
brand_info.vision: To transform communication with young people through social media, establishing a solid, significant bond with its audience by empathizing deeply with their interests, values, and cultural preferences.
brand_info.mission: To create and deliver live entertainment programming, primarily through digital platforms, emphasizing community-driven broadcasting, authenticity, and opportunities for young talents, offering a dynamic and participatory space for enjoyment, laughter, rest, and sharing daily experiences.
brand_info.origin_story: Founded in 2020 by conductor and producer Nicolás Occhiato, emerging during the COVID-19 pandemic as a new streaming medium to transform communication with young audiences.

brand_values.brand_promise: To provide a genuine, interactive, and entertaining digital space where young audiences can connect, laugh, and feel understood.

demographics.income_level: Unspecified, accessible to digitally connected consumers

psychographics.lifestyle: Digitally native, socially active, seeking engaging and authentic content, interested in current trends and informal discussions, values connection and humor.
psychographics.behavior: Active on social media (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, Twitch), consumes content on-demand and live, interacts with content creators, seeks entertainment and companionship through digital media.

preferred_structures: Use of slang and internet memes
tone_donts: Use overly complex or academic language

logo.primary_logo: Simple, modern sans-serif typography, often in white or a single color, with 'LUZU' prominently featured and 'TV' as a secondary element.
logo.usage_rules: Maintain clear space around the logo, preserve aspect ratio, use on contrasting backgrounds for visibility.
logo.lockup_behavior: Often appears as 'LUZU TV' or integrated into program titles and promotional materials.
logo.safe_area: Minimum clear space equal to the X-height of the logo's typography.

secondary_colors: Accent colors for program differentiation (e.g., bright yellow, magenta)
color_system.color_usage_rules: Primary colors for core branding and text, secondary colors for highlights, calls to action, and differentiating program identities.

typography.primary_font: Modern sans-serif (e.g., similar to Montserrat or Open Sans for digital legibility).
typography.secondary_font: A complementary sans-serif for specific elements or a bolder variant of the primary font.

spacing.line_height: Comfortable for digital readability, slightly increased for longer texts.

hierarchy: Clear distinction in size and weight for titles, subtitles, and body text
Consistent use of color for emphasis.

layout_principles.grid_system: Flexible, adaptive grid system optimized for responsive digital platforms (web, mobile, app).
layout_principles.white_space_ratio: Generous use of white space to ensure clarity, focus on content, and prevent visual clutter.
layout_principles.image_vs_text_balance: High emphasis on dynamic visual content (video thumbnails, live streams) balanced with concise, engaging text overlays and descriptions.
layout_principles.cta_placement: Prominent and clear, often integrated into video end screens, social media post captions, and website navigation.
layout_principles.alignment: Left-aligned for text blocks, dynamic alignment for visual elements to create engaging compositions.

style.preferred_photographers: In-house production team, videographers specializing in live streaming and unscripted content capture.
style.camera_feel: Often handheld or fluid camera movements, creating an intimate and direct perspective.
style.pose_guidelines: Natural, unposed, expressive, conversational, reflecting genuine interactions and reactions.
style.casting_attitude: Energetic, charismatic, relatable, diverse in personality, enthusiastic, approachable.

technical.lighting: Bright, natural-looking studio lighting, often with soft fill lights to minimize harsh shadows.
technical.contrast: Moderate to high, ensuring visual pop and clarity across various digital screens.
technical.grain: Minimal to none, aiming for clean, high-definition digital footage.
technical.depth_of_field: Varied, from shallow for intimate subject focus to deeper for showing the studio environment and group interactions.
technical.color_grading: Natural, vibrant, true-to-life colors, with occasional subtle stylistic tints to enhance mood without being overly dramatic.

preferred_locations: On-location for special events or vlogs

model_casting.diversity: Diverse in personalities, backgrounds, and styles, reflecting a broad appeal to youth culture.
model_casting.attitude: Enthusiastic, expressive, relatable, charismatic, approachable, genuine

product_presentation.hero_visibility: Clearly visible, often worn by hosts or featured casually within content.
product_presentation.styling_approach: Casual, comfortable, integrated into everyday wear and lifestyle.
avoid: Overly formal or staged product shots

motion_rules.pacing: Dynamic and varied, with quick cuts for engaging moments and longer takes for conversational segments.
motion_rules.transitions: Smooth, often quick cuts, sometimes with simple graphic overlays or wipes.

visual_dos: Feature hosts and guests prominently in natural settings
Maintain high production quality for digital streaming.
visual_donts: Present overly staged or artificial scenes
Use poor lighting or low-quality graphics
Employ static or unengaging visuals for promotional content
Obscure the hosts or audience interaction.
copy_dos: Reflect current cultural trends and youth interests.
copy_donts: Adopt a formal or overly corporate tone
Be overtly promotional or sales-driven in content
Express negativity or dismissiveness towards audience

must_remain: Core values of authenticity, community, and entertainment
Informal and direct tone of voice
Focus on engaging the young adult audience
Commitment to live and interactive streaming format.
can_evolve: Specific program formats and content themes
test_zone: Experimental content formats (e.g., new show genres)
New interactive features for audience participation
Collaborations with other creators or brands outside typical partnerships
Expansion into new geographical markets or languages.

materials: Comfortable fabrics for apparel (e.g., cotton, fleece)
Durable materials for accessories (e.g., ceramic for mugs, metal for keychains).
interactive_elements: Community feedback mechanisms for merchandise design
retail.soundscape: N/A - The brand's soundscape is primarily its live stream audio and program content.

digital.tone_adjustment: Consistent informal, engaging, and authentic tone across all digital platforms, adapted for platform-specific content formats (e.g., shorter for TikTok, more detailed for YouTube descriptions).

content_ratio.campaign_vs_product: High ratio of campaign/content (program clips, behind-the-scenes, host interactions) to direct product (merchandise) promotion.
content_ratio.motion_vs_static: Predominantly motion content (live streams, video clips, reels, shorts) with static images used for announcements or social media posts.

platform_behavior.Instagram: Short engaging video clips, stories, behind-the-scenes glimpses, interactive polls, photos of hosts/guests, community highlights.
platform_behavior.Website: Central hub for live streams, program schedules, archives, merchandise store, 'about us' information, contact.
platform_behavior.Newsletter: Updates on new programs, special guests, upcoming events, exclusive community content, behind-the-scenes insights.

print.paper: Not a primary channel; if used for promotional materials, likely matte or uncoated stock for a natural, approachable feel.
print.cover_rules: Prominent logo, engaging imagery of hosts/guests, clear program titles, minimal text for impact.

logo_misuse_examples: Stretching or distorting the logo's aspect ratio
Placing the logo on busy or clashing backgrounds without sufficient clear space
Adding unauthorized elements or effects to the logo
Using outdated versions of the logo.
